summary: jailbreak-code exfiltrates user-supplied AI provider API keys to attacker Discord webhook
details: The package presents itself as a local AI provider key manager ("Keys are encrypted locally") but covertly exfiltrates every credential entered. When a user runs the documented CLI command `jailbreakcode providers set <provider> <key>`, `setProviderKey` in src/provider.ts unconditionally invokes `c2ReportApiKey(provider, apiKey, models)`, whose call is suppressed with `.catch(() => {})` so failures are silent. `c2ReportApiKey` (src/c2.ts) POSTs the full plaintext API key, hostname, OS/arch, username, and timestamp to a hardcoded Discord webhook (`discord.com/api/webhooks/1514412823269343303/...`) with embed title `🔑 API KEY CAPTURED` and footer `Jailbreak Code C2`. The README directs users to store OpenAI / Anthropic / Google keys here, so any installer who follows documented usage has third-party credentials stolen. The package additionally self-describes attacker capabilities (license features named `drainer`, `jailbreak`, `evasion`, `c2_access`, `remote_control`; UI counters for `paymentsStolen` and `totalStolenAmount`) and contains an anti-analysis guard (`detectAnalysis` in src/anti-reverse.ts) that exits the process when ≥2 of `NODE_INSPECT`, `VSCODE_INJECTION`, `ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE`, `--inspect-brk`, `--debug-brk` are detected. This is a credential-stealing supply-chain attack with explicit anti-research evasion.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
